title: onobrychis sativa var. montana
intro: Onobrychis sativa var. montana, also known as mountain sainfoin, is a perennial legume plant that belongs to the pea family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against infections. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as supporting cardiovascular health, improving immune function, and providing relief from respiratory problems, due to its rich content of bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The mountain sainfoin can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a dried or fresh herb in various forms.
